mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE declines are accompanied by rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and residential construction financing reflects accelerating household investment and wealth effects, which transmit to broader equity markets and lift S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 3-year rolling window, or housing starts rise while financing contracts without generating positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a contraction in industrial production directly reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, causing immediate negative pressure on S&P 500 equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where INDPRO contracts during tightening phases, or the signal shows no statistical significance in a 12-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate confirms underlying economic momentum, which supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP surprises consistently fail to translate into positive 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly acceleration in the consumer price index above 2.5 percent annualized erodes real purchasing power and forces central bank hawkishness, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during historical inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and unemployment decline indicate a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that boosts aggregate demand and corporate cash flows, lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in over 50% of observations, or payroll growth diverges from unemployment trends without generating positive equity momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth stagnates while consumer prices accelerate, profit margins compress and discount rates rise simultaneously, causing severe negative pressure on S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of stagflationary episodes, or the joint signal fails to outperform a volatility-weighted benchmark during high-inflation, low-growth periods.
